title: Postmortem - Incident #3891: API Gateway Outage due to Memory Leak
content:
Postmortem - Incident #3891: API Gateway Outage due to Memory Leak

### Incident Summary
On March 28, the api-gateway experienced gradual degradation and eventual outage due to RAM exhaustion. The pods were OOMKilled by Kubernetes.

### Root Cause
A memory leak in the log formatting library caused objects to pile up in heap memory. As incoming load grew, heap capacity was exceeded.

### Resolution
Triggered a rollout restart of the deployment to recycle the pods, clearing RAM immediately. The log library was updated to the latest stable version in the next release cycle.

### Action Items
- Configure Prometheus alert to fire when memory usage > 85% for more than 10 minutes.
- Set memory limits in kubernetes pod definition.
